title: Track instances of the #error preprocessor directive being reached
description: Track instances of the "#error" preprocessor directive being reachedAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?This rule creates a issue whenever an#errorpreprocessor directive is reached during the project’s analysis. In most cases, this indicates that the preprocessor was badly configured. Some predefined macros or library include paths might be required to fix the configuration.Noncompliant code example#error This is an errorAvailable In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
#ifndef REQUIRED_MACRO
#error "Required macro REQUIRED_MACRO is not defined"
#endif

#if !defined(PLATFORM_WINDOWS) && !defined(PLATFORM_LINUX)
#error "Unsupported platform"
#endif

// Unconditional error
#error "This code is not ready for production"
compliant:
#ifndef REQUIRED_MACRO
#warning "REQUIRED_MACRO is not defined, using default"
#define REQUIRED_MACRO 1
#endif

#if !defined(PLATFORM_WINDOWS) && !defined(PLATFORM_LINUX)
#warning "Platform not explicitly supported, assuming POSIX"
#define PLATFORM_POSIX
#endif

// Use conditional compilation without error
#ifdef PRODUCTION_BUILD
    // Production code
#else
    // Development/debug code
#endif

checks.message: #error preprocessor directive reached. This indicates a compilation error condition.
